description_long: This house features a prominent hip roof covered in gray asphalt shingles, giving it a classic and sturdy appearance. The exterior walls are primarily black, with two sides displaying white-framed rectangular windows and one side featuring a central entryway with a wooden door. A distinctive decorative band with Indigenous art motifs is visible on one facade, adding cultural character to the structure. The house sits on a simple foundation with no visible porch or steps, and the windows are symmetrically arranged on the longer sides. The overall design is compact and single-storied, with clean lines and minimal ornamentation aside from the art panel. The materials suggest a modern construction with traditional influences.
description: A single-story house with a hip roof, black exterior walls, white-framed windows, and a decorative Indigenous art panel on one side.
description_view_0: The view shows the hip roof's peak and shingled surface, with no windows or doors visible.
description_view_1: This side displays two white-framed windows and a central entryway with a wooden door, set against black walls.
description_view_2: The facade features a decorative Indigenous art panel beneath the shingled roof, with no windows or doors visible.
description_view_3: This side has two symmetrically placed white-framed windows on a black wall, with no entryway or decorative panel.
attribute_entryway_design: Central wooden door with no porch or steps, flanked by windows and minimal ornamentation
